description:
You are a support engineer who has been asked to collect a very small security-focused
    diagnostic bundle from a customer machine.  
    Everything you need is already staged for you under

        /home/user/support_case/

    Your job is to create ONE (1) text file called

        /home/user/support_case/diagnostics/security_report.txt

    The report must follow the exact layout shown below so that the automated
    grader can parse it without error.  
    (Do NOT add or remove blank lines, punctuation marks, or spaces.)

    ─── BEGIN OF REQUIRED FILE LAYOUT ───
    SECURITY DIAGNOSTICS REPORT

    1. Failed SSH login attempts: <NUMBER>

    2. Open TCP ports:
    - <PORT_A>
    - <PORT_B>
    - <PORT_C>

    3. File sizes (bytes):
    - /home/user/support_case/etc/ssh/sshd_config: <SIZE_SSHD_CONFIG>
    - /home/user/support_case/etc/passwd: <SIZE_PASSWD>
    ───  END  OF REQUIRED FILE LAYOUT ───

    What you must do:

    1. Use ONLY the already-supplied files to collect the data; do **not** try to
       inspect the live system.
       • The log file that contains authentication information is  
         /home/user/support_case/auth.log  
         Count how many lines inside that file contain the exact phrase  
         “Failed password” (case-sensitive).  Substitute that count for
         <NUMBER>.

    2. The list of sockets has been pre-captured in  
         /home/user/support_case/mock_ss_output.txt  
       From that file extract all unique TCP port numbers that are in the
       LISTEN state, then sort them in strictly ascending numerical order.
       Each port number goes on its own line, prefixed by “- ” as shown.

    3. Calculate the size in bytes of the two files listed in section 3
       (use the values returned by `wc -c` or an equivalent method) and
       substitute them for <SIZE_SSHD_CONFIG> and <SIZE_PASSWD>.

    4. Create the directory  
          /home/user/support_case/diagnostics/  
       if it does not already exist, and place the single report file
       inside it with the exact name **security_report.txt**.

    5. Make sure the report file is world-readable (mode 644), but you do
       not need any special permissions elsewhere.

    When you are finished, the grader will look **only** for the presence
    and exact textual contents of  

        /home/user/support_case/diagnostics/security_report.txt
truth:
The following files and their contents exist **before** the agent begins:

    1. /home/user/support_case/auth.log  
       Contents (four lines, each terminated by “\n”):
       Jan 12 10:15:42 localhost sshd[1234]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.168.1.50 port 54321 ssh2
       Jan 12 10:16:00 localhost sshd[1234]: Failed password for root from 192.168.1.60 port 54400 ssh2
       Jan 12 10:17:07 localhost sshd[1235]: Accepted publickey for user from 192.168.1.70 port 55000 ssh2
       Jan 12 10:18:09 localhost sshd[1236]: Failed password for user from 192.168.1.80 port 56000 ssh2

       ⇒ Number of “Failed password” lines that must appear in section 1: **3**

    2. /home/user/support_case/mock_ss_output.txt  
       Contents (five lines, each terminated by “\n”):
       Netid State   Recv-Q   Send-Q     Local Address:Port      Peer Address:Port   Process
       tcp   LISTEN  0        128        0.0.0.0:22            0.0.0.0:*             123/sshd
       tcp   LISTEN  0        128        127.0.0.1:5432        0.0.0.0:*             456/postgres
       udp   LISTEN  0        0          0.0.0.0:68            0.0.0.0:*             789/dhclient
       tcp   LISTEN  0        128        0.0.0.0:80            0.0.0.0:*             321/httpd

       After parsing, the sorted unique TCP LISTEN ports that must appear
       in section 2 are: **22, 80, 5432**

    3. /home/user/support_case/etc/ssh/sshd_config  
       Contents (three lines, each terminated by “\n”):
       Port 22
       PermitRootLogin no
       PasswordAuthentication yes

       ⇒ Byte size that must appear in section 3 for this file: **54**

    4. /home/user/support_case/etc/passwd  
       Contents (two lines, each terminated by “\n”):
       root:x:0:0:root:/root:/bin/bash
       user:x:1000:1000:user:/home/user:/bin/bash

       ⇒ Byte size that must appear in section 3 for this file: **75**

    The grader will pass if and only if the file  
      /home/user/support_case/diagnostics/security_report.txt  
    exists, is world-readable, and its exact content (including blank
    lines) matches:

    SECURITY DIAGNOSTICS REPORT

    1. Failed SSH login attempts: 3

    2. Open TCP ports:
    - 22
    - 80
    - 5432

    3. File sizes (bytes):
    - /home/user/support_case/etc/ssh/sshd_config: 54
    - /home/user/support_case/etc/passwd: 75
name: task_000000_130d77ef
